articles.title: GOP senators delay immigration funding. And, what to expect this hurricane season
articles.summary: Congressional Republicans left for recess without passing Trump's top immigration enforcement package. And, forecasters are predicting fewer storms than average for the 2026 hurricane season.

articles.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? The best options for saving gas
articles.summary: The national average for a gallon of gasoline is $4.55, as America heads into one of the busiest travel weekends of the year. What can you do to cut your costs?

articles.summary: Nearly three months since the fighting began, disagreements remain over the fate of Iran’s uranium stockpile and transit fees for the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Asian Currencies Hit by Iran War, Adding to Economic Strain
articles.summary: Soaring oil prices and a surging dollar are testing Asia’s foreign-exchange reserves, which were built up after the 1997 crisis.

articles.title: Palestinians drop bid for a senior U.N. role after U.S. pressure

articles.summary: They played second fiddle to private equity and hedge funds for years, but 2026 is shaping up to be “the year of the bank,” one consultant said.

articles.title: A stranger's compliment helped her feel confident after chemo
articles.summary: When Pat Gentile began to grow out her hair after chemotherapy, she was nervous to go to work for the first time without a wig. An unexpected encounter with a convenience store stranger changed that.

articles.title: UN gravely concerned by an Afghan Taliban law that has provisions on child marriage
articles.summary: The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.

articles.summary: President Trump faced a wall of opposition from Senate G.O.P. lawmakers, in part over his plan to create a $1.8 billion fund to reward his allies.

articles.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
